articles.title: Flaws in Kenya’s AI-driven health reforms driving up costs for the poorest
articles.description:
Exclusive: amid unrest, President William Ruto promised to give all Kenyans access to healthcare. But the algorithm favours the rich, an investigation has found


 An AI system used to predict how much Kenyans can afford to pay for access to healthcare, has systemically driven up costs for the poor, an investigation has found.
The healthcare system being rolled out across the country, a key electoral promise of President William Ruto, was launched in October 2024 and intended to replace Kenya’s decades-old national insurance system.

articles.title: Three passengers dead after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
articles.description:
Dutch husband and wife and third unidentified person reported to have died, with three further people taken ill
A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus infection on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean killed three people – including an elderly married couple – and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organisation (WHO) and South Africa’s health department said on Sunday.
The WHO said an investigation was under way but that at least one case of hantavirus had been confirmed. One of the patients was in intensive care in a South African hospital, the UN’s health agency said in a statement to the Associated Press, and the WHO was working with authorities to evacuate two others with symptoms from the ship.
 Continue reading...

articles.description: President Trump calls the Iran blockade unbelievably effective as U.S. forces seize ships carrying oil worth millions, sending a message to Russia and China.

articles.title: Inside China’s AI ‘wolf pack’ drones built with Taiwan conflict in mind
articles.description: China is developing AI-powered robotic wolf packs designed to scout and support troops in a potential Taiwan invasion, according to an Foundation for Defense of Democracies report.

articles.title: We can't let RI's 'assault weapons' ban become the US gun seizure blueprint
articles.description: Rhode Island's SB 2710 targets semi-automatic rifles, pistols and shotguns while leaving enforcement unclear, raising fears of forced confiscation.

articles.title: The oldest millennials are 45! This tool helps plan for longevity
articles.description: The oldest millennials are turning 45 this year. The oldest Gen Xers are now 60. So how prepared will they be to thrive in the decades ahead? A new tool helps people assess beyond just savings.

articles.title: In many states, election-denying candidates are running to control voting
articles.description: In 23 states, including five presidential swing states, candidates who've denied election results are running for offices that have a direct role in certifying future elections, a new report finds.

articles.title: After NPR investigation, new bill aims to stop 'claim sharks' targeting disabled vets
articles.description: The bipartisan bill would block companies from using auto-dialers to harvest private information about disabled veterans. The firms charge vets for a service lawmakers say is supposed to be free.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu poll rumours trigger disturbing act: TVK chief Vijay’s fan attempts suicide in Krishnagiri
articles.description: A 28-year-old man in Tamil Nadu’s Krishnagiri allegedly slit his throat after hearing unverified rumours about actor-politician Vijay’s electoral defeat. He is currently undergoing treatment, police said.

dailyNews.title: ‘Results will flip after sunset’: Mamata Banerjee claims TMC is leading in 100 more seats, alleges ‘BJP’s plan’
dailyNews.description: Mamata Banerjee alleges manipulation at counting centres, with malfunctioning machines and threats aimed at Trinamool Congress. Despite challenges, she insists her party leads in many seats and encourages workers to remain patient, predicting a turnaround in results.

dailyNews.title: Iowa woman died following hernia repair after nurses dismissed painful post-surgery symptoms: lawsuit
dailyNews.description: The family of an Iowa woman who died after hernia repair surgery alleges nurses dismissed fecal drainage and sepsis symptoms as normal in medical malpractice lawsuit.

dailyNews.description: Spokesman says Giuliani in 'critical but stable' condition as President Trump hails 'true warrior'.

dailyNews.title: Tuning out: Why many Americans are sick of the news – especially Trump news
dailyNews.description: About six in 10 Americans try to avoid news about Trump, a new poll finds, reflecting widespread news fatigue amid a flood of negative headlines.

dailyNews.title: Assam Assembly election results 2026: BJP leads in around 80 seats; key candidates status
dailyNews.description: The BJP is aiming for a third consecutive term in Assam, while the Congress is attempting a comeback after a decade out of power

dailyNews.title: India is burning more coal as extreme heat and the Iran war squeeze energy supplies
dailyNews.description: India, the world's third-largest emitter of carbon dioxide, is burning more coal as demand for power rises, most of which is coal-fired.

dailyNews.title: 2 US service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
dailyNews.description: Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) said.

dailyNews.title: Trump announces new effort to escort ships out of Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: President Trump announced on Sunday that Project Freedom, an effort to escort ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz, will begin Monday.

dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump said he ordered U.S. forces to guide ships and crews from countries that are not involved with the Iran war out of Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: The nuclear option: Atomic energy could offer Europe hope, say analysts — but it won't be easy
dailyNews.description: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route, has exposed which nations are most exposed to disrupted energy imports.

dailyNews.description: At least 10 people have been taken to hospitals following a shooting at party at Arcadia Lake, near Oklahoma City, police say.

dailyNews.title: Trump says the U.S. will ‘free up’ ships in the Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: President Trump says the U.S. will start escorting ships through the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, after he didn’t rule out a return to fighting with Iran. The president also told an Israeli media outlet that Iran’s latest peace offer was “not acceptable.” NBC News’ Raf Sanchez has the latest.

dailyNews.title: Trump Faces the Complicated Reality of a Costly, Unpopular War in Iran
dailyNews.description: President Trump’s predictions of a relatively short-term conflict with minimal economic consequences appear to be crumbling.

dailyNews.title: Cameron Young says it's an 'honor' to play in front of President Trump after win at Cadillac Championship
dailyNews.description: Cameron Young secures a dominant six-shot victory at Trump National Doral with President Trump in attendance, beating Scottie Scheffler by six strokes.

dailyNews.title: Aziz Ansari's Kash Patel calls himself 'first Indian person to suck at their job' in 'SNL' cold open
dailyNews.description: Aziz Ansari and Colin Jost opened SNL playing Kash Patel and Pete Hegseth in a White House press briefing sketch satirizing the administration.

dailyNews.title: A Long, Strange Trip: How the G.O.P. Came to Embrace Psychedelic Drugs
dailyNews.description: For decades, conservatives were adamantly opposed to the use of drugs like psilocybin and LSD. Now, the Trump administration has made a sharp pivot.

dailyNews.title: In a Reversal, Doctors From Countries Under Trump’s Travel Ban Are Allowed to Stay in U.S.
dailyNews.description: In a reversal, the Trump administration has exempted foreign physicians from a visa application freeze that was pushing many out of jobs in underserved areas.

dailyNews.title: Smog in Phoenix and Salt Lake City? The E.P.A. Is Blaming Asia.
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration says the cities shouldn’t be penalized for unhealthy air because pollution can blow in from abroad. Some experts say that’s preposterous.

dailyNews.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after failed effort at government rescue deal
dailyNews.description: The budget carrier Spirit Airlines is ceasing operations after failing to land a $500 million bailout from the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: Trump's poll numbers tank as Iran war continues to jack up gas prices
dailyNews.description: Prolonged uncertainty about the war in Iran coupled with rising energy and grocery prices are pummeling pocketbooks and President Trump's polling numbers. Olivia Gazis reports.

dailyNews.title: Man arrested at Trump Miami golf club for disturbance, Secret Service says
dailyNews.description: The incident occurred one week after shots were fired during the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington, D.C., and Mr. Trump was rushed off the dais.

dailyNews.title: Oil prices rise as Trump plans to 'free' ships stranded due to Mideast conflict
dailyNews.description: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations agency said Monday that a tanker was hit by projectiles north of the city of of Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates.

dailyNews.title: European markets open mostly positive as Trump threatens new auto tariffs
dailyNews.description: European markets began the new trading week in largely positive territory, as investors watched Middle East developments.
